canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, FashionMNIST is consistently described as a dataset composed of Zalando's article images, with a training set of 60,000 examples and a test set of 10,000 examples. The dataset is used for the task of image classification. One paper specifies its role in a training pipeline, where it is used for fine-tuning, while the MNIST dataset is used for pretraining in the same context. Another source characterizes FashionMNIST as a 'drop-in replacement for MNIST'.
papers: Beyond Zero Initialization: Investigating the Impact of Non-Zero Initialization on LoRA Fine-Tuning Dynamics
DTZO: Distributed Trilevel Zeroth Order Learning with Provable Non-Asymptotic Convergence
Learngene Tells You How to Customize: Task-Aware Parameter Initialization at Flexible Scales

definitions.text: A dataset of Zalando's article images, consisting of a training set of 60,000 examples and a test set of 10,000 examples, used for image classification.
definitions.paper: DTZO: Distributed Trilevel Zeroth Order Learning with Provable Non-Asymptotic Convergence

definitions.text: A dataset of Zalando's article images, consisting of a training set of 60,000 examples and a test set of 10,000 examples, used as a drop-in replacement for MNIST.
definitions.paper: Beyond Zero Initialization: Investigating the Impact of Non-Zero Initialization on LoRA Fine-Tuning Dynamics
